meta.name: Go Kart Knowledge Base
meta.description: Structured documentation for the Go Kart low-code enterprise application framework — enriched with real template, field rule, and web pilot patterns

supplementalFiles.templateExamples: docs/template-examples.json — 10 real templates (prospect, kanban, credential vault, etc.)
supplementalFiles.templateFragments: docs/template-fragments.json — 11 reusable fragments (base, prospect_general, kanban_board_card, etc.)
supplementalFiles.webPilotExamples: docs/web-pilot-examples.json — Full NC Auto web pilot chain (11 scripts)

overview.description: Go Kart is a low-code enterprise web application framework built with Go. It enables rapid development of database-driven applications through configuration stored in MongoDB collections rather than traditional code.

techStack.frontend: HTMX, Hyperscript, Wave CSS web components

keyPrinciples: Configuration over code — apps defined via MongoDB collections, not source files
Template-driven UI — screens are HTML templates with embedded JavaScript logic
Schema-driven data — JSON Schema (via LiteSpec DSL) defines validation, types, and UI
Template composition — {% include 'slug' %} for reusable fragments across screens
Field rules for interactivity — declarative client-side form behavior (visibility, sync, calculate)
HTMX for dynamic updates — partial page rendering without full reloads
Hyperscript for inline event handling — _="on click ... end" syntax
Web Pilots for automation — composable Playwright scripts with {% include %} reuse

requestFlow: 2. Global middleware: Recoverer → Logger → Timeout → CORS → Session → CSRF
3. Route-specific middleware: Auth (for /x/ routes) → WithRequestDetails → ValidateRoutePrefix
4. WithRequestDetails loads: user, apps, template + depends, schema, lookups into context
5. Handler extracts slug and record_id from URL
6. Handler executes template's JavaScript code (runGet/runPost/runPut/runDelete) via Goja VM
7. JavaScript uses rdx.ConnName/rdx.DBName for DB access, form.* for submitted data
8. Template rendered with Pongo2 using returned data — {% include %} fragments resolved
9. If HTMX request (HX-Request header), wraps in partial-base; otherwise full base template

protected.description: Authenticated routes under /x/ — same patterns as /v/ but require login

api.tabulatorData: GET /api/{collection} — paginated, sortable, filterable data for wc-tabulator

middleware.name: VaryHXRequest
middleware.purpose: Cache partial (HTMX) and full page separately

middleware.name: Auth
middleware.purpose: Redirect unauthenticated users to login (for /x/ routes)

middleware.name: APIKeyAuth
middleware.purpose: Validate X-API-Key header for programmatic API access

middleware.name: WithRequestDetails
middleware.purpose: Load apps, templates + depends, schemas, lookups into request context

templateSystem.description: Templates are the primary building blocks. Each has HTML content, JavaScript logic, optional field rules, and supports composition via {% include %}.

templateTypes.standard: Full page screens — forms, lists, dashboards (74 in production)
templateTypes.fragment: Reusable partial content included by other templates (40 in production)
templateTypes.base: Layout wrappers — base, partial-base, login_base, email_base (7 in production)
templateTypes.email: Email templates rendered with record data (23 in production)
templateTypes.data: Data-only endpoints returning JSON (1 in production)

composition.description: Templates support composition via {% include %} and {% extends %} for code reuse
composition.extends: {% extends "__template_name__" %} — Every standard template extends a base template. The base is resolved at render time.
composition.blocks: {% block pageContent %}...{% endblock %} — Define content regions within base templates. Also: {% block css %}, {% block title %}
composition.include: {% include "slug" %} — Include another template's content inline. The included template has access to the same context variables.
composition.conditionalInclude: {% if condition %}{% include "slug_a" %}{% elif other %}{% include "slug_b" %}{% endif %}
composition.depends: The 'depends' field lists template slugs that must be loaded. Common pattern: ['base', 'loader', 'partial-base']. The system also auto-detects {% include %} references.
composition.realExample: The 'prospect' template includes 5 fragments: meta_fields, prospect_general, prospect_member, prospect_vehicle, prospect_rental_info. Each fragment is a self-contained UI section that can be maintained independently.
composition.kanbanExample: The 'kanban_board' template includes 'kanban_board_card' inside a {% for %} loop — same card fragment rendered for each ticket in each lane.

codeTab.description: Server-side JavaScript executed in Goja VM. Define lifecycle functions. Use rdx.ConnName and rdx.DBName for database access (preferred over raw session calls).

functions.runGet: Executed on GET requests — load record and related data for display
functions.runPost: Executed on POST requests — create or update records from form submission
functions.runPut: Executed on PUT requests — update existing records from form submission
functions.runDelete: Executed on DELETE requests — remove records or array items
functions.runList: Executed on GET /list — load multiple records for table view

contextObject.rdx.ConnName: Current database connection name (PREFERRED — shorthand for session lookup)
contextObject.rdx.DBName: Current database name (PREFERRED — shorthand for session lookup)
contextObject.ctx.Request: HTTP request object (access URL, headers, query params)
contextObject.ctx.RecordID: Current record ID from URL path
contextObject.ctx.RecordIndex: Array item index for delete operations
contextObject.ctx.ExtractQueryParams(keys): Extract URL query params as override map for CreateNewWithOverrides
contextObject.form.*: Form field values from POST/PUT body (e.g., form.first_name, form.email)
contextObject.ObjectIDFromHex(id): Convert string ID to MongoDB ObjectID for queries

databaseOperations.Find: ctx.DB.Find(rdx.ConnName, rdx.DBName, collection, filter, sort, projection, skip, limit)
databaseOperations.UpdateByID: ctx.DB.UpdateByID(rdx.ConnName, rdx.DBName, collection, id, data, arrayFields)
databaseOperations.CreateNew: ctx.DB.CreateNew(rdx.ConnName, rdx.DBName, schemaSlug) — create blank record from schema defaults
databaseOperations.CreateNewWithOverrides: ctx.DB.CreateNewWithOverrides(rdx.ConnName, rdx.DBName, schemaSlug, username, overrides) — create with query param overrides
databaseOperations.SaveAndValidate: ctx.DB.SaveAndValidate(rdx.ConnName, rdx.DBName, collection, id, form, schemaSlug, tagFields, arrayFields) — validate against schema before saving

contentTab.description: HTML template markup using Pongo2 (Django/Jinja2 syntax). Uses Wave CSS components for UI.

pongo2Syntax.conditional: {% if Record.status == "active" %}...{% elif Record.status == "pending" %}...{% else %}...{% endif %}

contentTab.formPattern: {% include "meta_fields" %}
contentTab.dynamicAddPattern: Add Item

htmxPatterns.deleteItem: hx-delete="/x/slug/{{ RecordID }}/delete/{{ index }}?array_field=field_name" hx-headers='{"X-CSRF-Token": "{{ CSRFToken }}"}' hx-swap="none"
htmxPatterns.navigation: hx-get="/x/slug/{{ record._id|objectid }}" hx-target="#viewport" hx-swap="innerHTML transition:true" hx-push-url="true"

hyperscriptPatterns.confirmDelete: _="on click set result to wc.Prompt.question({'title': 'Confirm Remove?', 'text': 'Are you sure?'}) if result trigger 'confirm:delete' end end on htmx:afterRequest if detail.successful then set elem to the closest <.item/> transition the elem's opacity to 0 then settle then remove the elem end"
hyperscriptPatterns.setActive: _="on click remove .active from <.item.active/> add .active to closest .item"

templateData.description: Data available in Pongo2 template rendering context

fields.Record: Current record data (bson.M) — access nested fields: Record.address.street
fields.Data: Additional data from code tab return — e.g., Data.referrals, Data.scripts
fields.Template: Current TemplateBuilder — Template.RoutePrevTemplateSlug for back navigation
fields.Lookups: Map of lookup name → lookup data
fields.CSRFToken: CSRF protection token — required in hx-headers for DELETE requests
fields.FormMethod: HTTP method lowercase — used in hx-{{ FormMethod }} for dynamic verb
fields.QueryParams: URL query parameters — use {{ QueryParams|getparam:"key" }} and {{ QueryParams|querystring }}

generation.formGeneration: GenerateTemplateBySchema() — creates form HTML with Wave CSS components from schema properties
generation.tableGeneration: GenerateTableBySchema() — creates wc-tabulator with columns from schema properties
generation.schemaGeneration: GenerateSchema() — creates blank record with default values from schema

fieldRules.description: Declarative client-side form behavior engine. Controls field visibility, required state, value syncing, calculations, dynamic options, and more. Defined in template's field_rules JSON property.
fieldRules.location: Stored in _template_builder.field_rules as JSON string. Parsed and initialized by FieldRulesEngine on page load.

targeting.byName: "field_name" — targets input with name="field_name" and its wc-* wrapper
targeting.byClass: ".section-class" — targets elements with that CSS class
targeting.byWildcard: ".member-title-*" or "household_members.*.first_name" — wildcard * matches array indices for repeating sections

visible.description: Show/hide field or section based on condition

required.description: Make field required based on condition — same syntax as visible

clearWhenHidden.description: Clear field value when it becomes hidden (boolean)

syncWith.description: Bidirectional sync between fields. Changes to either field update the other.
syncWith.notes: Uses dot notation for nested/array fields. Index 0 = first array item.

setValue.description: Set field value based on condition. Supports copyFrom for copying another field's value.
setValue.notes: Supports compound 'and' conditions with field/equals/in/isEmpty checks

setText.description: Set element text content from field values using template string

calculate.description: Calculate and set values on target fields when source field changes
calculate.notes: Wildcard * in field paths matches the same array index as the source field

updateOptions.description: Dynamically rebuild select/dropdown options from an array field's data
updateOptions.notes: Rebuilds options whenever the source array changes. Useful for assigning drivers to vehicles.

conditionOperators.isEmpty: Check if field is empty — "isEmpty": false

realWorldExample.description: From the prospect template — shows syncWith, setValue with copyFrom, compound conditions, wildcard targeting, calculate, updateOptions, and setText
realWorldExample.syncFields: first_name syncs with household_members.0.first_name (and last_name, middle_initial, suffix)
realWorldExample.mailingAddress: When mailing_same_as_physical='yes', all mailing_address.* fields copyFrom address.* fields
realWorldExample.liabilityLimits: When state is NC/SC and bi limit is set, umbi copies from bi with copyOnChange
realWorldExample.memberTitles: .member-title-* uses setText with wildcard to show 'First Last' for each household member
realWorldExample.driverAssignment: household_vehicles.*.assigned_driver uses updateOptions from household_members array

webPilots.description: Browser automation system using Playwright. Scripts are stored in _web_pilot collection. Support composition via {% include %} and data loading via code tab — same patterns as templates.

architecture.collection: _web_pilot — stores script definitions with 'script' and 'code' fields
architecture.runCollection: _web_pilot_run — execution history with status, logs, screenshots
architecture.executor: External Node.js Playwright service at PW_EXECUTE_URL

scriptStructure.code: Pre-execution JavaScript — loads data, credentials, and sets up context object before script runs. Uses same DB operations: load(), loadMany(), getCredentials().
scriptStructure.script: Playwright automation code — uses page.goto(), page.fill(), page.click(), page.screenshot(), etc. Supports Pongo2 template syntax for data injection.
scriptStructure.composition: {% include 'page_script_slug' %} — include other web pilot scripts inline. {% if data.state == 'NC' %}{% include 'nc_version' %}{% elif data.state == 'SC' %}{% include 'sc_version' %}{% endif %}

scriptPatterns.lookupMapping:
const mappedValue = context.mapSourceToTarget('coverage_category', sourceValue);
await page.selectOption('#field', mappedValue);

compositionExample.description: The nat_gen orchestrator script demonstrates the full composition pattern — loads data in code tab, then conditionally includes page scripts based on state
flow: 1. Code tab: Load prospect data, carrier lookup, credentials → context object
2. Script: Navigate to carrier login URL
3. Auth check: Race between authenticated element and login button
7. Continue through: drivers → vehicles → underwriting → coverages → bill_plans
8. Screenshots after each page for audit trail
compositionExample.totalScripts: 88 web pilot scripts in production, organized by carrier and page

executionConfig.data_id: Source record ID to automate against

app.description: Top-level organizational container grouping related templates with navigation and permissions

fields.name: Display name
fields.permissions: Array of permission strings for access control

navItem.name: Display name
navItem.slug: Template slug (typically the list view slug)

templateBuilder.description: Screen/page definition with HTML template, JavaScript logic, and field rules

fields.name: Display name
fields.slug: URL identifier — used in routes: /x/{slug} or /v/{slug}
fields.schema: Reference to schema slug for data validation
fields.collection_name: Primary MongoDB collection this template operates on
fields.collections: Array of additional MongoDB collection names
fields.lookups: Array of lookup names for dropdown data
fields.depends: Array of dependent template slugs — typically ['base', 'loader', 'partial-base']
fields.route_prev_template_slug: List view slug for back navigation
fields.is_core: Boolean — core system template vs user-created

schemaBuilder.description: Data schema definition — JSON Schema with UI extensions. Created via LiteSpec DSL or manual JSON.

fields.name: Display name
fields.slug: Unique identifier (referenced by template's schema field)
fields.json_schema: JSON Schema definition (stored as JSON string)

user.description: User account with authentication and permissions

userProfile.description: Role configuration with permissions and custom navigation

fields.name: Schedule name
fields.execution_config: Object with username, playwright_env, headless, debug, data_collection, data_id

system._web_pilot: Web pilot script definitions (88 in production)

mongodbCollections.notes: Custom business data collections are user-defined. Referenced in template 'collections' field. Each collection can have its own schema via _schema_builder.

simpleFormScreen.description: A basic CRUD form screen for editing a single record
simpleFormScreen.content: {% extends '__template_name__' %} with {% block pageContent %}, wc-breadcrumb, wc-form with hx-{{ FormMethod }}, wc-input/select/textarea fields, {% include 'meta_fields' %}, wc-save-split-button
simpleFormScreen.code: runGet() with CreateNew for 'create' or FindByID for existing. runPut() with SaveAndValidate. runPost() same as runPut. runDelete() for record or array item removal.
simpleFormScreen.fieldRules: Conditional visibility/required, syncWith for related fields, setValue with copyFrom

listScreen.description: A table view showing multiple records with sorting, filtering, and pagination
listScreen.content: wc-breadcrumb, 'Add New' button with hx-get to /x/{slug}/create, wc-tabulator with wc-tabulator-column children. Link formatter on primary column.
listScreen.code: runList() is typically minimal — TabulatorApi at /api/{collection} handles data loading automatically.

composedFormScreen.description: A complex form with tabbed sections, each tab including a reusable fragment
composedFormScreen.example: prospect template — includes prospect_general, prospect_member (repeating), prospect_vehicle (repeating), prospect_rental_info
composedFormScreen.content: wc-tab with wc-tab-item wrapping {% include %} fragments. Dynamic add buttons with hx-get to fragment/create, hx-target='.container', hx-swap='beforeend'
composedFormScreen.fragments: Each fragment is a standalone template with its own content (and optionally code). Fragments render inline within the parent form.

kanbanBoard.description: A drag-and-drop kanban board with lanes and cards
kanbanBoard.content: Lanes as div.kanban-lane with data-status, card fragment included in {% for ticket in Data.status_list %} loop
kanbanBoard.code: runGet() loads tickets grouped by status into Data.backlog, Data.current_sprint, etc. runDelete() removes tickets.

webPilotAutomation.description: Multi-page browser automation composing page scripts with state-conditional logic
webPilotAutomation.orchestrator: Main script loads data in code tab, navigates to carrier URL, conditionally includes page scripts based on data state/type
webPilotAutomation.pageScripts: Self-contained page automation — fill forms, select options, click buttons, screenshot
webPilotAutomation.lookupMapping: mapSourceToTarget() function maps internal data values to carrier-specific form values using _carrier_* collections

steps: 1. Create Schema via LiteSpec DSL — define fields, types, validation, UI properties
2. Create Template in _template_builder — set slug, schema, route prefix, depends: ['base','loader','partial-base']
3. Write Content Tab — {% extends '__template_name__' %}, {% block pageContent %}, Wave CSS components, {% include %} fragments
4. Write Code Tab — runGet() with rdx.ConnName/rdx.DBName, runPut() with SaveAndValidate, runDelete()
5. Define Field Rules — syncWith, setValue/copyFrom, visible/required conditions, calculate, updateOptions
6. Create fragments for reusable sections — meta_fields, repeating items, shared UI blocks
7. Create List Template — wc-tabulator, link formatter columns, Add New button
8. Create App — group form + list templates as NavItems with permissions
9. Create Lookups for dropdown data in _lookup collection
10. Test: /x/{slug}/create for new record, /x/{slug}/{id} for existing, /x/{slug}_list/list for table

conventions.slugNaming: entity_name for form, entity_name_list for list view
conventions.routePrefix: Use /x/ for authenticated screens (most common), /v/ for public-facing
conventions.queryParams: Pass state context via query params: ?address.state=NC&quote.insurance_type=auto
conventions.metaFields: Always {% include 'meta_fields' %} in forms for created_by/modified_by tracking

envVariables.PW_EXECUTE_URL: Playwright service URL for web pilots
